title: Patriots’ Harold Landry placed on physically unable to perform list
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: New England Patriots Landry missed spring practices and has yet to be cleared for training camp.
Harold Landry didn't practice on Thursday due to a knee injury.
(AP Photo/Charles Krupa) Patriots edge rusher Harold Landry has been placed on the physically unable to perform list.

title: Pedestrian fatally struck by tractor-trailer on I-95 in N.H.
author: John Waller
bullets: Local News The incident happened Tuesday morning.
By John Waller July 22, 2026 | 10:49 AM Less than a minute to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.
A pedestrian was fatally struck Tuesday morning by a tractor-trailer on Interstate 95 in Seabrook, New Hampshire, officials said.

title: Health officials investigating possible harmful algae bloom in the Charles River after dog’s death
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News The Boston Public Health Commission is warning residents to avoid the water between Kendrick Street in Needham and Spring Street in West Roxbury after a dog died earlier this week.

Jessica Rinaldi / The Boston Globe, File The Boston Public Health Commission is warning residents to avoid a stretch of the Charles River while state officials investigate a possible harmful cyanobacteria bloom following the death of a dog.
The advisory covers the river between Kendrick Street in Needham and Spring Street in West Roxbury, including areas near Millennium Park and Cutler Park Reservation.

title: Here’s what Boston meteorologists are saying about the possibility of ‘strong thunderstorms’ Wednesday
author: John Waller
bullets: Local News "Should get the games in at Fenway today.
" By John Waller July 22, 2026 | 8:51 AM 1 minute to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.

National Weather Service: ‘There will be the risk for isolated strong thunderstorms between 1 & 8 pm this evening, but this activity is not expected to be widespread’ [Isolated Thunderstorm Risk Today Between 1-8 PM] It will be partly sunny, warm and humid today.

title: BU signs $104M PILOT agreement with city, the largest in Boston history
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News This is BU's first PILOT agreement in more than 25 years.
People walk through Boston University's campus near Marsh Chapel.
Jessica Rinaldi/The Boston Globe Boston University and the city of Boston have finalized the largest payment-in-lieu-of-taxes (PILOT) agreement in the city’s history, officials said.

title: Logan Airport sees hundreds of delays and cancellations due to thunderstorms
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Transportation More than 300 flights were canceled and more than 400 flights were delayed, with rain expected to continue throughout the night.
JetBlue and Avianca planes sit at Terminal E gates at Boston Logan International Airport.
Danielle Parhizkaran/Globe Staff Boston Logan International Airport is seeing hundreds of delays and cancellations Tuesday night amid a thunderstorm.
